original: The World Wide Web is first proposed by CERN computer scientistsTim Berners-Leeand Robert Cailliau
European Space Agency's Rosetta lands the Philae probe on the comet 67P/Churyumov-Gerasimenko
Buzz Aldrintakes the first 'space selfie', a photo of himself performing extravehicular activity in space during the Gemini 12 mission
Scientists perform 1st artificial synthesis of a live cell
Space western TV series set in the Star Wars franchise "TheMandalorian" byJon Favreauis released on Disney+

Doc and Marty McFly are sent "Back to the Future" when lightning strikes the Hill Valley clock tower, at exactly 10:04 p.m. generating the power to send the DeLorean and Marty back to 1985 [fictional]
Jack Ryan, American toy designer, inventor, and engineer at Mattel (Barbie Doll, Hot Wheels, Chatty Cathy), born in Yonkers, New York (d. 1991)
Michael Ende, German fantasy and children's writer (The Neverending Story), born in Garmisch-Partenkirchen, Bavaria (d. 1995)
First commercial bungy jumping company begins operating near Queenstown, New Zealand, run by A. J. Hackett
Jules Leotard performs 1st Flying Trapeze circus act (Paris) He also designed garment that bears his name
Space shuttle astronauts snared a satellite 1st space salvage
US space probe Voyager I approaches 77,000-mi (124,000 km) of Saturn
Shanghai Transrapid sets a new world record for commercial railway systems with a speed of 501 km/h (311 mph)
The great Leonid Meteor shower recorded
xQc [Félix Lengyel], Canadian YouTuber and Twitch streamer, born in Laval, Quebec
Kathleen Herles, American child voice actress (Dora the Explorer, and Go, Diego, Go! - "Dora"), born in Queens, New York
Disney launches its film and television streaming service Disney+
1st balloon crossing of Pacific is completed (Double Eagle V)
2nd shuttle mission-1st time spacecraft launched twice (Columbia 2)
Lawrence Hargrave, Australian aeronautical pioneer and inventor of the box kite, linked four huge box kites together and flew - but remained attached to the ground by piano wire
First known photo of so-called Loch Ness monster is taken by Hugh Gray
Largest observed iceberg, 208 by 60 miles, 1st sighted
Paula Murphy sets female land speed record 226.37 MPH
A salmon is discovered in the River Thames, England, for the first time since 1833
First possible movie stunt: A man jumps into the Hudson River from a burning balloon
Alma Heflin, 1st female test pilot for commercial aircraft - Piper Corporation, Lock Haven, Pennsylvania
Unprecedented heatwave in Brazil affects more than 3,000 towns with Rio de Janeiro reaching 42.5C - very high humidity makes it feel like 58.5C
Andrew Ellicott makes the first known record of a meteor shower observation in the U.S, from a ship off the coast of Florida Keys
Venera 2 launched by Soviet Union toward Venus
British explorerRobert Falcon Scott's diary and body are found in Antarctica
"Orbital" by British writer Samantha Harvey, set on the International Space Station, wins the 2024 Booker Prize for fiction
Sir James Young Simpson, a British physician, is the first to use chloroform as an anaesthetic
Tim Samaras, American tornado chaser (Storm Chasers), born in Lakewood, Colorado (d. 2013)
Jacques Charles [Jacques-Alexandre-César Charles], French inventor and balloonist, (with Nicholas Robert, was the first to take flight in a hydrogen balloon), born in Beaugency, France (d. 1823)
Meeting of the Royal Society appointsJames Cookas head of an expedition to the Pacific Ocean to observe the transit of Venus on Tahiti in 1769 (will be Cook's 1st voyage aboard HMS Endeavour)
Ross & Keith Smith start a 1 month flight from London to Australia
US Supreme Court: Epperson v. Arkansas declares unconstitutional the Arkansas law banning the teaching of evolution in public schools
Exposition Universelle (World's Fair) in Paris closes after 50 million visitors - Art Nouveau style dominates
Taylor Swiftreleases her directorial debut the short film "All Too Well" alongside her re-recorded album "Red (Taylor's Version)"
Jackie Chanawarded honorary Oscar for his film achievements at 8th Annual Governors Awards ceremony
Brian Hyland, American rocker ("She Wore an Itsy Bitsy Teeny Weeny Yellow Polka-Dot Bikini"), born in Queens, New York City
Booker T. Jones, American soul and rock organist (Booker T & MGs - "Green Onions"), born in Memphis, Tennessee
Best-selling American novel "Ben-Hur: A Tale of The Christ" by soldier Lew Wallace is published
Opera "Silent Night" by Kevin Puts opens at the Ordway Theater, St. Paul sung in English, German, French, Italian and Latin (winner 2012 Pulitzer Prize for Music)
New Zealand's Black Ferns win Women's World Rugby Championship defeating England 34-31 at Eden Park in Auckland in front of a capacity crowd
Guillaume Cizeron, French ice dancer (Olympic gold [Gabriella Papadakis] 2022; World C'ship gold 2015–16, 18–19, 22), born in Montbrison, Loire, France
Irina Privalova, Russian athlete (Olympic gold 400m hurdles 2000; silver 4×100m, bronze 100m 1992), born in Malakhovka, Russia
Aaron Pryor scores a 14th round KO of Alexis Argüello in Miami to retain his WBA super lightweight boxing title in the first of 2 meetings between the Hall of Famers; The Ring’s 'Fight of the Decade'
German Benetton driverMichael Schumachercrashes in the season-ending Australian Grand Prix in Adelaide but claims second straight F1 World Drivers Championship by 33 points from Damon Hill
Novak Đokovićof Serbia wins his second season-ending ATP World Tour Finals tennis title beating 6-time championRoger Federer7-6, 7-5 in the final in London. England
1st NFL football game on a Sunday at Philadelphia's Baker Bowl - sporting events on Sunday had been illegal on Sundays; Eagles tie Chicago Bears, 3-3
Marino breaks Tarkenton's NFL all-time passing yardage mark of 47,003
The SS Robert E. Peary, a Liberty ship built in 4 days and 15.5 hours atHenry J. Kaiser's Richmond Shipyard #2, is launched, setting a new record
Salim Ali, Indian ornithologist 'Birdman of India', born in Bombay, British India (d. 1987)
Seth Barnes Nicholson, American astronomer who discovered four satellites of Jupiter, born in Springfield, Illinois (d. 1963)
Audouin Dollfus, French Astronomer (discovered Janus, a moon of Saturn), born in Paris, France (d. 2010)
Welton Taylor, American microbiologist, inventor and civil rights activist (developed tests for salmonella), born in Birmingham, Alabama (d. 2012)[1]
Archibald "Moonlight" Graham, American MLB baseball outfielder (New York Giants - 1 game, 1905), medical doctor, and subject of "Field of Dreams" film, born in Fayetteville, North Carolina (d. 1965)
Theodore W. Richards is 1st American to win Nobel Prize in Chemistry
Ambrose Godfrey patents a gunpowder fueled fire extinguisher system in England
